quote_of_day.text:
>>23071
Why are you hung up on lost years. Let it go. You end up where you were always meant to be. Our genes determine how our brain works and thinks. We don't get to choose external circumstances either. Free will is an illusion. There's no point in having regrets.
quote_of_day.name: Himachali gunda

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A toxic low-pressure system of caste-based hatred dominated the morning, with a post calling for the genocide of a specific group sparking a dense fog of controversy that lingered for hours.

This dark front brought scattered showers of abusive language and intense debate.

A heat wave of optimism about IIT placements and AI's impact rolled in during the late morning, with Anon engaging in a lively discussion about the intersection of technology and employment.

Meanwhile, a cold snap of disillusionment with Indian politics and society settled over a portion of the board, with one poster lamenting the widespread loss of faith in the system.

The afternoon saw a high-pressure ridge of youthful energy, as an inquiry about the presence of young oldfags on the board sparked a brief but intense exchange.

A scattered drizzle of caste baiting continued to plague parts of the discussion, refusing to clear up.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Genocide of LCs, Discussion Ensues
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post calling for the genocide of all LCs, stating 'All LCs should be killed
Unka genocide hona chahiye'.

Another anon responded by listing various castes, including Adi-Dharmi, Kachhandha, Agria, and several others. One anon questioned the OP, asking them to name all LC castes in response to the initial call for their killing.

Another anon asked if 'ponga lives' mattered too. The conversation took a turn when an anon mentioned their college flirt was from a 'chhoti jaati' they thought was Baniya.

Anon also brought up that 'avg converted gangoo ponga doesn't matter'. Some anons engaged in banter, with one saying 'Grow up kiddo' and another calling someone the 'Smartest 3rd gen inch user'.

Anon also made derogatory remarks about certain castes, stating 'Only choti jati is narrows minded and slow wit people'.

The discussion also touched on Rajputs, with one anon saying they 'fought all the time' and 'gave their women to Mughals because they wanted to fight all the time'.

The thread remained on topic, with several anons contributing their thoughts and opinions on the subject.

articles.title: IIT placements and AI impact
articles.body:
Anon started a discussion on IIT placements this year and the impact of AI on them, specifically asking if AI affected IIT placements and mentioning 'IITchads'.

Anon argued that IITians are valued primarily for their JEE ranks, not for any exceptional technical knowledge gained during their education. They claimed that IITs lack sufficient funding for cutting-edge research and that their technical education is not particularly special.

One anon mentioned that IIT Bombay's median CTC is around 16-18LPA. Another anon countered that IITians' ability to grind and their JEE ranks make them valuable.

They also claimed that institutions like MIT, UC Berkeley, and Caltech have significantly more funding than all 23 IITs combined. The conversation turned to the topic of startups and hard tech R&D in India.

Anon stated that the startup meme popularized in the mid-2010s has died down, and that India is 30-50 years behind foreign competitors in these areas. They expressed skepticism about building a SpaceX-like company in India.

Anon also discussed the priorities of IITs, questioning why they focus on placements over research in areas like space tech and weapons. They criticized the Indian government's and corporations' lack of investment in hard tech R&D.

The discussion also touched on the topic of companies rescinding job offers. Anon mentioned that Oracle and IITs may blacklist certain companies from placements. Another anon shared a story about Flipkart rescinding job offers to 5 people with 1 Cr CTC.

The conversation was marked by skepticism about the value of IIT education and the prospects of hard tech startups in India.

articles.title: Caste Baiting Sparks Controversy
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion, stating that banning a certain user would make 90% of caste baiting disappear.

Another anon, Himachali gunda, responded with a list comparing perceived insecurities across different groups, including low castes being more conscious of caste. Anon questioned the original poster's stance, directly asking about their caste.

This line of questioning was echoed by another anon who accused the OP of deflecting and demanded to know their caste.

Accusations flew back and forth, with multiple anons labeling the OP as a low caste individual themselves. One anon claimed the OP was making caste bait in every thread.

The conversation remained fixated on caste, with anons directly asking about the OP's and the mod's caste. Some referenced specific caste labels, such as Dalit.

The discussion did not move beyond these exchanges, with no clear resolution or shift in topic.

articles.title: Anon laments widespread disillusionment with Indian politics, society
articles.body:
Anon observed that almost everyone has become disillusioned with Indian politics, citing a decrease in Modi supporters on social media. They noted this disillusionment extends beyond politics to economy and society.

Another anon attributed India's societal issues to the COVID-19 pandemic, citing stagnated economic metrics and decreased consumption. A respondent invoked Swami Karpatri, suggesting democratic governments are inherently corrupt.

One anon mentioned the Ram Mandir's corruption issues, with 200 crore rupees vanishing. A reply blamed Brahmin priests for the corruption.

articles.title: Youngcel oldfag presence queried
articles.body:
Anon asked if any youngcel oldfag from Rusty's Inch, who isn't a NEET, is present on Indiachan. They are about to finish college and wondered if others like them from 2020-2023 are still around.

One respondent claimed to have been on IBs until 2023 and 2025's first quarter. Another mentioned being on inch since February 2024. A third anon referenced a 'kaleshi aurat sariya case migrant', seemingly in response to the query.

The reason for people's broken connections was questioned, with possibilities of paranoia or superiority complex floated.
